rulings.Case Title: IBRAHIM MOHAMMED VS. LEADING INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Leading Insurance Services, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike And Or Tax Plaintiffs Costs California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 998c And 1033.5 And California Rule Of Court Rule 3.1700
rulings.Rulings: Hearing Required. The outcome of this motion will turn in part on the Court's determination of the new trial motion and any further reductions that would be appropriate pusuant to CCP section 998 if plaintiffs recovery were to remain below the amount of the 998 offer. Even if the new trial motion is granted, however, the Court would tax all costs other than the jury fees and juror lunches because plaintiff has not carried its burden as to them. In addition, the Court would tax 40% of the allowed costs because that amount was already recovered in the judgment. Therefore, all costs would be taxed except that 60% of 3,033.46, which equals $1,820.08, would be allowed. Hearing will be held at 2:00 p.m. Friday, January 23, 2015, in Department 318 of the Civic Center Courthouse.

rulings.Case Title: IBRAHIM MOHAMMED VS. LEADING INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Hearing required. Given that the Court initially denied fees based on its interpretation of the contract between plaintiff and its attorneys and that the parties to that contract have now presented parol evidence supporting an interpretation pursuant to which plaintiff has incurred an obligation to pay fees, should the Court reconsider its prior ruling and determine that plaintiff is entitled to recover attorney fees incurred in proving entitlement to contract damages arising out of defendant's bad faith denial of coverage? If so, should the Court award such fees in the amount of $100,000 indicated in its tentative ruling prior to the intitial hearing? Hearing will be held at 2:00 p.m. Friday, January 23, 2015 in Department 318 of the Civic Center Courthouse.

rulings.Case Title: IBRAHIM MOHAMMED VS. LEADING INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike And Tax Defendants Memorandum Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: Hearing required. The outcome of this motion turns in large part on the Court's determination of the motion for new trial. If the new trial is granted and the Court awards attorney fees to plaintiff, defendant would not be entitled to any costs. If the new trial is denied, the Court would be inclined to allow the supported amount of $18,851.44, but to tax the discretionary costs of $36,649.10. The hearing will be held at 2:00 p.m. Friday, January 23 in Department 318.
